chemistry_vs_rest.note: S4 ligands are indistinguishable from the rest in size/flexibility: drug-like, not tiny metals nor giant peptides.

parseability.verdict: All 76 have valid SMILES + RDKit descriptors + is_proper=True => the ligands DO parse; NaN similarity is NOT a per-ligand fingerprint parse failure.

similarity_block_lockstep.verdict: For 74/76 S4 instances the ENTIRE similarity block (ligand morgan+topological, pocket_qcov, protein_seqsim) is NaN together. Missing similarity = the RNP/PLINDER similarity SEARCH returned no training neighbor at all, not a ligand-only issue.

fallback_topological.verdict: topological_tanimoto is NaN in lockstep with morgan (only 2/76 S4, and only 2/1287 in the whole set differ). It is NOT a usable fallback; no in-file re-stratification rescues S4.

pipeline_artifact_signals.verdict: Missing-similarity is strongly entangled with multi-ligand-chain systems (97% of morgan-NaN in full set are multi-chain vs 53% when present; 53% of S4 is multi-component). ~18% of S4 are ubiquitous cofactors/sugars (ADP/AMP/FAD/NAP/UDP/NAG...) that certainly HAVE training analogs -> their NaN is pipeline coverage, not true novelty.

difficulty.note: S4 is genuinely harder (44% vs 67% correct), consistent with real novelty in at least part of the stratum.

clean_genuine_novelty_core.definition: single ligand chain AND single proper ligand chain AND non-cofactor/sugar (drug-like, monomeric)

verdict_3line: S4 is a MIXED stratum, not a clean no-analog set: every ligand parses and is drug-like, but 'missing similarity' means the PLINDER similarity SEARCH returned no neighbor for the WHOLE system (ligand+pocket+protein all NaN in lockstep), and this is heavily entangled with multi-ligand-chain systems (53% of S4; 97% of all morgan-NaN rows) and ~18% ubiquitous cofactors/sugars that plainly DO have training analogs.

There is NO in-file fallback: topological_tanimoto is NaN in lockstep with morgan (recovers 2/76), so S4 cannot be re-stratified without recomputing ECFP4/pocket similarity externally (PLINDER/RDKit) for the ~29 single-component drug-like members.

Bottom line: the abstract's 'no-analog tail is uncertifiable' claim is DIRECTIONALLY supported (S4 is 44% vs 67% accurate, genuinely hard) but OVER-STRONG as stated -- roughly half of S4 is a multi-component / cofactor similarity-pipeline coverage gap rather than confirmed genuine novelty; recommend either recomputing similarity to purify S4 or softening the claim to 'systems with no computable training analog', reporting the ~29-instance drug-like core separately.
